work_unit: SZ-BG-H-003: tile ownership boundary fix in mergeOcrResults
situation: mergeOcrResults in ocr-internal.ts used global midpoint (midX/midY) to assign OCR results to tiles via quadrant check. Tiles overlap by 30% (tile size = 70%, stride = 30%). The global midpoint falls inside the overlap zone, causing text blocks near image center to be dropped from the correct tile and potentially lost entirely.
action: Replaced quadrant-based ownership check (tileOwnsLeft/tileOwnsTop vs resultInLeft/resultInTop against midX/midY) with tile actual bounds check. Each tile now keeps results whose center falls within [tile.x, tile.x+tile.w) × [tile.y, tile.y+tile.h). Overlap-zone duplicates are resolved by downstream nmsDedup call. Removed unused scaledW/scaledH/midX/midY variables.
outcome_evidence: Commit 64a0fcb on auto/opt-background-1783213292. Diff: -10 lines (quadrant logic + unused vars), +9 lines (bounds check + comment). Branch pushed to origin. Stacked on prior SZ-BG-H-001/H-002 fixes in same worktree.
next_suggestion: Next HIGH priority pending fix: SZ-SP-H-001 (containerRef dual assignment in SidePanelApp.tsx) or SZ-PP-H-001 (upload size limit in ImageUploader.tsx). Both are route-fixes in different areas requiring new worktrees.

audit_findings.finding: Tile ownership quadrant check uses global midpoint instead of tile actual bounds, dropping valid OCR results near tile boundaries in the 30% overlap zone
audit_findings.remediation: Replace quadrant check with tile bounds check; rely on NMS dedup for overlap-zone duplicates
